questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What must you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Stop only if other cars are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires a complete stop behind the stop line or crosswalk until the light turns green.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so prepare to stop
Speed up to beat the red light
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that the signal is about to turn red, so you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a flashing red traffic light?
choices: Treat it as a stop sign
Treat it as a yield sign
Wait for it to turn green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means stop completely, then proceed when it is safe, just like a stop sign.

questions.prompt: In Connecticut, when may you turn right at a steady red light?
choices: After a full stop and yielding, unless a sign prohibits it
Only when an officer signals you
questions.explanation: Connecticut allows a right turn on red after a complete stop and yielding to pedestrians and traffic, unless a sign forbids it.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is broken, so stop
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed carefully, watching for cross traffic.

questions.prompt: What shape and color is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: Stop signs are always red and octagonal so they can be recognized even when partly blocked.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign generally means what?
choices: A warning of a hazard ahead
A speed limit change you may ignore
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs warn of hazards such as curves, intersections, or crossings ahead.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow center line on your side of the road mean?
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is not allowed because it is unsafe there.

questions.prompt: What color are construction and work zone signs?
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and work zones where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: When a police officer is directing traffic at a signaled intersection, whom do you obey?
choices: The police officer, even if it conflicts with the signal
questions.explanation: Directions from a police officer always take priority over traffic signals and signs.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who proceeds first when two vehicles arrive at the same time?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians in the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What must you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on?
choices: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the area
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a marked or unmarked crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Connecticut drivers must yield to pedestrians crossing in marked or unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When entering a road from a private driveway, what should you do?
choices: Stop and yield to all traffic and pedestrians
Expect traffic to stop for you
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roadway from a driveway must yield to all traffic and pedestrians already there.

questions.prompt: At a roundabout, who has the right-of-way?
choices: Traffic already circulating in the roundabout
questions.explanation: You must yield to vehicles already in the roundabout and enter only when there is a safe gap.

questions.prompt: When two vehicles meet on a narrow mountain road where only one can pass, who generally yields?
questions.explanation: The driver facing downhill should yield because it is easier to back up and reverse uphill.

questions.prompt: Before making any turn, what should you do first?
questions.explanation: Signaling early warns other drivers and pedestrians of your intended turn so they can react safely.

questions.prompt: From which lane should you normally begin and end a right turn?
choices: As close as practical to the right curb
questions.explanation: Right turns should begin and end close to the right edge to avoid conflicts with other traffic.

questions.prompt: When merging onto a highway from an on-ramp, you should do what?
choices: Match the speed of highway traffic and merge into a gap
Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Merge slowly and force traffic to slow
questions.explanation: Accelerating to match traffic speed lets you merge smoothly into a safe gap.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in Connecticut cities and towns?
questions.explanation: In Connecticut, the default speed limit in built-up city and town areas is 25 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: What is the typical maximum speed limit on most rural interstate highways in Connecticut?
questions.explanation: Most rural interstates and freeways in Connecticut have a maximum posted speed limit of 65 mph.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum recommended following distance under good conditions?
questions.explanation: A three-second following distance gives you time to react and stop safely in good conditions.

questions.prompt: Even if you are below the posted limit, you can still be ticketed for what?
questions.explanation: You must reduce speed for weather, traffic, and road conditions even when below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance to four seconds or more?
choices: In rain, fog, or at higher speeds
When you are in a hurry
questions.explanation: Poor visibility and higher speeds lengthen stopping distance, so you need a larger space cushion.

questions.prompt: The best way to handle a curve is to do what?
choices: Slow down before entering the curve
Brake hard while in the curve
questions.explanation: Slowing before the curve keeps your tires gripping and avoids skidding while turning.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it can do what?
choices: Block traffic flow and cause crashes
Have no effect on other drivers
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly disrupts traffic and can lead to rear-end and passing crashes.

questions.prompt: On rural roads with no posted speed limit, the Connecticut default speed is approximately what?
questions.explanation: Rural roads in Connecticut without a posted limit default to about 50 mph.

questions.prompt: What is a good practice for maintaining a space cushion?
choices: Avoid driving in another vehicle's blind spot
Tailgate to keep up with traffic
Drive in groups bumper to bumper
questions.explanation: Staying out of blind spots keeps you visible and gives you room to maneuver.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, the left lane is mainly intended for what?
questions.explanation: Keep right except to pass; Connecticut promotes a 'Pass Left, Drive Right' practice for safe traffic flow.

questions.prompt: When is it unsafe and illegal to pass another vehicle?
choices: On a curve or hilltop where you cannot see ahead
When a broken yellow line is on your side
questions.explanation: You must never pass where your view of oncoming traffic is blocked, such as curves and hills.

questions.prompt: Before returning to your lane after passing, you should do what?
choices: See the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Return as soon as you pass the front bumper
Wait for the other driver to wave you in
questions.explanation: Seeing the whole vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to return safely.

questions.prompt: What does a broken yellow center line mean?
choices: You may pass when it is safe
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line means passing is permitted on your side when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What must you do before changing lanes?
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check your blind spot
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show blind spots, so you must signal and look over your shoulder before moving over.

questions.prompt: What does a solid white lane line indicate?
questions.explanation: A solid white line separates lanes going the same direction and discourages crossing it.

questions.prompt: When should you generally not attempt to pass?
choices: When you have a clear view
questions.explanation: Passing near intersections and crossings is dangerous because of turning and crossing traffic.

questions.prompt: If another driver is passing you, what should you do?
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly
Speed up to prevent the pass
questions.explanation: Holding your speed or easing off lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: On a one-way street, the left edge lane is reserved for what?
choices: Faster traffic and the leftmost flow of vehicles
questions.explanation: On a one-way street all lanes move the same direction, and traffic should keep right except to pass.

questions.prompt: How much space should you give when passing a bicyclist in Connecticut?
choices: Only enough to fit your mirror
questions.explanation: Connecticut law requires drivers to leave at least three feet of space when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Under Connecticut's Move Over law, what must you do when approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Slow down and move over a lane when possible
Maintain speed and stay in your lane
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires slowing below the limit and changing lanes away from stopped emergency or disabled vehicles when safe.

questions.prompt: When you see a school bus stopped with red lights flashing and its stop arm out on an undivided road, what must you do?
choices: Stop in both directions until the lights stop flashing
Stop only if you are behind the bus
questions.explanation: On an undivided road, all traffic must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights until it begins to move.

questions.prompt: Why should you give motorcycles a full lane width?
choices: They can stop quickly and need room to maneuver
They must stay in the center only
questions.explanation: Motorcycles need a full lane to maneuver and avoid hazards, and they can stop or swerve quickly.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have big blind spots known as what?
questions.explanation: No-Zones are areas around a truck where the driver cannot see you, so avoid lingering there.

questions.prompt: Why should you not cut in front of a large truck closely?
choices: Trucks need much more distance to stop
It is legal and safe to do
questions.explanation: Loaded trucks require a much longer stopping distance, so cutting in front of them is dangerous.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you must do what?
choices: Expect them to yield to you
questions.explanation: A pedestrian who is blind has the right-of-way, and you must stop and yield to them.

questions.prompt: When driving near a work zone, you should do what?
choices: Slow down and watch for workers and equipment
questions.explanation: Work zones require reduced speed and extra caution to protect workers and avoid sudden hazards.

questions.prompt: How should you treat a bicyclist riding on the road?
choices: As a legal road user with the right to a lane
As an obstacle to push past
As someone who must use only the sidewalk
As a vehicle that never has rights
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the same right to use the road and must be given safe space and respect.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for drivers age 21 and over in Connecticut?
questions.explanation: In Connecticut, a driver 21 or older is legally intoxicated at a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: What is the BAC limit for drivers under 21 under Connecticut's zero-tolerance law?
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face a zero-tolerance limit of 0.02 percent, and any measurable alcohol can mean suspension.

questions.prompt: What does Connecticut law say about hand-held cell phone use while driving?
choices: It is illegal for all drivers
It is allowed only on highways
It is allowed when stopped at a light
questions.explanation: Connecticut bans hand-held phone use and texting for all drivers, even when stopped in traffic.

questions.prompt: May a driver under 18 use a hands-free cell phone while driving in Connecticut?
choices: No, drivers under 18 may not use any phone
Yes, but only on local roads
Yes, if a parent is present
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 may not use any cell phone, including hands-free devices, while driving.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving ability?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions, impairs judgment, and reduces coordination, making driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: Under Connecticut's implied consent law, what happens if you refuse a chemical test?
questions.explanation: By driving you consent to testing, and refusing results in an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: Besides alcohol, what else can impair your ability to drive?
questions.explanation: Marijuana and many prescription or over-the-counter drugs can impair driving just like alcohol.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to avoid a distracted-driving crash?
choices: Put your phone away before driving
Use your phone with one hand
Hold the phone below the window
questions.explanation: Putting the phone away before you drive removes the temptation and keeps your focus on the road.

questions.prompt: If you plan to drink, the safest choice is to do what?
choices: Arrange a sober driver or other transportation
questions.explanation: Only time removes alcohol from your body, so plan a sober ride rather than driving impaired.

questions.prompt: In Connecticut, when you must use your windshield wipers, what else must be on?
questions.explanation: Connecticut law requires headlights on whenever wipers are needed for rain, snow, or fog.

questions.prompt: In dense fog, which headlights should you use?
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so low beams work better in fog.

questions.prompt: When is the road often most slippery?
choices: During the first rain after a dry spell
After several hours of heavy rain
questions.explanation: Oil and dust mix with the first rain to make roads especially slippery before heavy rain washes it away.

questions.prompt: If your car begins to skid, what should you do?
choices: Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and steering gently in your intended direction helps regain control in a skid.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your driving on wet or icy roads?
choices: Slow down and increase following distance
questions.explanation: Slick roads lengthen stopping distance, so reduce speed and leave extra space.

questions.prompt: Before driving after a snowstorm, what should you do?
choices: Clear snow and ice from all windows, lights, and the roof
Clear only the driver's side window
questions.explanation: Clearing all glass, lights, and the roof gives you full visibility and prevents ice from flying off.

questions.prompt: When are headlights required at night in Connecticut?
choices: From a half-hour after sunset to a half-hour before sunrise
Only when it is fully dark
Only when other cars are present
questions.explanation: Headlights must be on from a half-hour after sunset until a half-hour before sunrise.

questions.prompt: What is hydroplaning?
choices: Tires riding on a film of water and losing grip
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when water lifts the tires off the road, so slow down in heavy rain to prevent it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb lets the curb stop the car if it rolls forward.

questions.prompt: Connecticut seat belt enforcement is primary, which means what?
choices: An officer can stop you solely for not buckling up
You can only be ticketed after another violation
Only the driver must buckle up
questions.explanation: Primary enforcement means police may stop and ticket you just for a seat belt violation.

questions.prompt: In Connecticut, a child under two years old or under 30 pounds must ride in what?
questions.explanation: Children under two or under 30 pounds must be secured in a rear-facing child car seat.

questions.prompt: Where is it illegal to park?
choices: In front of a fire hydrant
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited in front of fire hydrants, crosswalks, and intersections for safety and access.

questions.prompt: Until what age or weight must a child generally use a child restraint or booster seat in Connecticut?
choices: At least 8 years old and 60 pounds
At least 5 years old and 30 pounds
questions.explanation: Children must use an appropriate restraint or booster until they are at least 8 years old and weigh 60 pounds.

questions.prompt: Before leaving your parked vehicle, you should do what?
choices: Set the parking brake and shift to park
questions.explanation: Setting the brake and shifting to park keeps the vehicle from rolling while unattended.

questions.prompt: When should you use your hazard (four-way) flashers?
choices: When your vehicle is disabled or stopped unusually
questions.explanation: Hazard flashers warn others that your vehicle is disabled or stopped in an unexpected place.

questions.prompt: What should you never do with a child or pet in a parked car?
choices: Leave them alone in the vehicle
Roll down a window for them
questions.explanation: Never leave a child or pet alone in a vehicle, where temperatures can quickly become deadly.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner's permit in Connecticut?
questions.explanation: In Connecticut you must be at least 16 to apply for a learner's permit.

questions.prompt: How many hours of behind-the-wheel training must a 16- or 17-year-old complete in Connecticut?
questions.explanation: Teen applicants must complete at least 40 hours of behind-the-wheel, on-the-road training.

questions.prompt: What is the nighttime driving curfew for newly licensed 16- and 17-year-olds in Connecticut?
questions.explanation: Newly licensed teens may not drive from 11 p.m. to 5 a.m. except for work, school, medical, or religious reasons.

questions.prompt: During the first six months of licensure, whom may a 16- or 17-year-old driver carry as passengers?
choices: Parents, a legal guardian, or one qualified adult
questions.explanation: For the first six months, only parents, a guardian, or one qualified licensed adult may ride along.

questions.prompt: While holding a learner's permit, you must always drive with what?
choices: A qualified licensed supervisor or instructor
Only a friend in the car
questions.explanation: Permit holders must have a licensed instructor or qualified adult supervising all driving.

questions.prompt: What does Connecticut's Graduated Driver License (GDL) system do?
choices: Phases in driving privileges as a new driver gains experience
questions.explanation: The GDL system gradually grants full driving privileges as teen drivers gain supervised experience.

questions.prompt: An applicant under 18 in Connecticut typically needs what to get a permit?
choices: Parental consent and proof of school enrollment
questions.explanation: Applicants under 18 need a parent or guardian's consent and proof they are enrolled in school.

questions.prompt: During months seven through twelve of licensure, who may ride with a 16- or 17-year-old driver?
choices: Members of the driver's immediate family
questions.explanation: During the second six months, only immediate family members may be passengers, in addition to those allowed earlier.

questions.prompt: What happens to a driver under 21 with a BAC of 0.02 or higher in Connecticut?
choices: License suspension under the zero-tolerance law
questions.explanation: Connecticut's zero-tolerance law suspends the license of an under-21 driver at a BAC of 0.02 or above.

questions.prompt: A first-time DUI conviction in Connecticut may include what?
choices: Fines, license suspension, and an ignition interlock device
questions.explanation: A first DUI can bring fines, license suspension, possible jail, and a required ignition interlock device.

questions.prompt: What is the consequence for a teen who violates GDL passenger or curfew restrictions?
choices: A free pass the first time
questions.explanation: Teen drivers who break passenger or curfew rules face suspension of their license.

questions.prompt: By driving in Connecticut, you give implied consent to what?
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs when lawfully requested
Paying any fine without a hearing
questions.explanation: Driving means you agree to chemical testing, and refusing leads to an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you accumulate too many points or serious violations?
questions.explanation: Accumulating too many points or serious offenses can lead to license suspension.

questions.prompt: What is a penalty for illegally passing a stopped school bus with flashing red lights?
choices: A fine and possible points or suspension
questions.explanation: Passing a stopped school bus is a serious violation that carries fines and possible license consequences.

questions.prompt: Using a hand-held cell phone while driving in Connecticut can result in what?
questions.explanation: Hand-held phone use is illegal and can result in a traffic fine that increases for repeat offenses.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of traffic laws and their enforcement?
choices: To keep all road users safe
questions.explanation: Traffic laws and their enforcement exist to protect the safety of everyone using the road.
